title: Iran military vows to 'soon respond' after US destroyer hits cargo ship
subhead: US President Trump announced that the Iranian vessel, Touska, ignored warnings to stop in the Gulf of Oman and the USS Spruance 'stopped them right in their tracks by blowing a hole in the engine room.' Iran's military, later on Sunday, vowed to 'soon respond and retaliate,' reported the ISNA news agency.

title: Israel vows to counter threats in Lebanon with 'full force, both on the ground and from the air'
Eight children dead in Louisiana domestic violence shooting, officials say
Bulgaria's former president Radev tops parliamentary vote
subhead: Rumen Radev has said he wants to rid the Balkan nation of its 'oligarchic governance model.' Projections from polling agencies put his Progressive Bulgaria grouping at some 44%, which would give him an absolute majority of at least 129 seats in the 240-seat parliament. Official final results are expected no earlier than Monday.

title: The undeniable international success of Languedoc wines
subhead: Appellations from the world's largest wine-growing region sell 40% of their production abroad. Thanks to strong ambassadors, wines from the area are now found in nearly half the countries on the planet.

title: 'Come Closer' by Tomora: When the Chemical Brothers' electronic fever meets Aurora's grace
subhead: British musician Tom Rowlands, one half of the Chemical Brothers, and Norwegian singer Aurora blend their musical worlds on their stunning first joint album.

title: From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
subhead: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Eric Dedier and  Rémi Ducasse

A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
subhead: Between fall 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

title: Louis Verret paints the drama of football in watercolor
subhead: The artist based in Aubervilliers, a suburb of Paris, paints football's masters as if they are freeze-frames, channeling pure emotion straight to the goal.

title: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
subhead: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.
